title: Minors (under 18) applying for citizenship - Canada.ca
content:
## Minors (under 18) applying for citizenship
There are 2 types of citizenship applications for minors: minor 5(2) and minor 5(1) . Make sure you know the difference and choose the right application for your situation.
PDF version: Minors applying for citizenship (PDF, 127.91 KB)
- a Canadian parent or
- a parent who is applying for citizenship at the same time
Yes for most applicants Apply for Canadian citizenship online .
Yes for most applicants
Apply for Canadian citizenship online .
Yes Use the application package for minor 5(2) .
Use the application package for minor 5(2) .
Are a permanent resident
Must pass the citizenship test and meet the language requirements
Must have been physically present in Canada
          3 out of the last 5 years
Must have filed income tax
Must take the Oath of Citizenship
Yes, if 14 years of age or older
Must sign the application
Yes A parent or legal guardian having custody of the minor must sign. Minors who are 14 or over must also sign.
- a Canadian parent or
- a parent who is applying for citizenship at the same time
Yes for most applicants Apply for Canadian citizenship online .
Yes for most applicants
Apply for Canadian citizenship online .
Yes Use the application package for minor 5(1) .
Use the application package for minor 5(1) .
Are a permanent resident
Must pass the citizenship test and meet the language requirements
Must have been physically present in Canada
          3 out of the last 5 years
Must have filed income tax
Yes, if needed Find out if the minor must file their taxes .
Yes, if needed Find out if the minor must file their taxes .
Must take the Oath of Citizenship
Yes, if 14 years of age or older
Must sign the application
Yes A parent, a legal guardian having custody of the minor or a person empowered to act on their behalf must sign. If there’s no one who can apply on their behalf, the minor can request a waiver (exemption) for this requirement . Minors who are 14 or over must also sign.
